summary: Updated the Linux build image to gc-wii-binutils 2.42-1 and replaced raw `.4byte` encodings of PowerPC `mftb` instructions with explicit `mftb` assembly syntax in MetroTRK and Dolphin OS reset assembly. `asm/dolphin/os/OSReset.s` was also re-addressed from section-relative labels/comments to linked-address labels/comments, causing a large but mostly mechanical 295-line rewrite.
what_changed: In `asm/MetroTRK/mpc_7xx_603e.s`, replaced two raw instruction words, `.4byte 0x7D4C42E6` and `.4byte 0x7D6D42E6`, with `mftb r10, 268` and `mftb r11, 269` while preserving the same opcodes in the comments.
In `asm/dolphin/os/OSReset.s`, replaced raw time-base reads `.4byte 0x7CAC42E6` and `.4byte 0x7CCC42E6` with `mftb r5, 268` and `mftb r6, 268`.

Rewrote `OSReset.s` labels and address comments from local zero-based labels such as `.L_000000A8` and range `# 0x00000000 - 0x000003D4` to linked-address labels such as `.L_803483B8` and range `# 0x80348310 - 0x803486E4`.
Updated `OSReset.s` displayed opcodes/relocations for linked-address context, including real branch displacements and `sda21` encodings in comments.
smart_moves: Paired the assembly source cleanup with a toolchain bump, indicating the new binutils release could assemble the desired `mftb` syntax instead of requiring opaque `.4byte` fallbacks.
Kept raw opcode comments beside the new `mftb` mnemonics, making it easy to verify that the semantic source cleanup did not change instruction bytes.

Used numeric time-base register operands (`268`, `269`) directly in `mftb` instructions, matching the assembler-supported syntax shown in the diff and avoiding speculative symbolic aliases not present in the PR.
Limited the functional assembly change to the previously opaque time-base reads; the large `OSReset.s` churn appears mechanical address/comment relabeling rather than broad logic edits.
decomp_lessons: When a PowerPC instruction is present only as `.4byte`, first check whether the project toolchain can be updated to support the canonical mnemonic before carrying raw words forward into decompiled assembly.

For `mftb` in this project slice, the accepted syntax is shown as `mftb rD, 268` or `mftb rD, 269`; future assembly cleanups should prefer that evidence-backed form over opaque `.4byte` when using the updated binutils.
Large assembly diffs can be mostly address/comment normalization. Reviewers and future agents should distinguish true instruction changes from label-address churn.
Preserving opcode comments next to mnemonic substitutions is a useful matching tactic: it documents that readability improved without changing the emitted instruction word.
naming_conventions: Linked-address local labels in `OSReset.s` use the `.L_8034....` form after this PR, replacing zero-based `.L_0000....` labels.
Assembly comments in re-addressed files use linked virtual addresses plus file/ROM offsets, for example `/* 803483B8 00344F98  7C AC 42 E6 */`.
The PR does not introduce new C symbols, structs, or headers; no struct naming convention evidence is present.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace raw words for known instructions with explicit mnemonics once the assembler supports them: `.4byte 0x7D4C42E6` became `mftb r10, 268`; `.4byte 0x7D6D42E6` became `mftb r11, 269`.
For OS reset timing loops, the time-base reads remained byte-identical while becoming readable `mftb` instructions.
Use a toolchain bump (`gc-wii-binutils` `2.42-1`) as an enabling step for assembly source fidelity when instruction parsing/printing support changes.
Treat generated address/comment changes separately from semantic code changes during match review.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other `.4byte` encodings in assembly that are now supported by gc-wii-binutils 2.42-1 and can be converted to mnemonics.
Search for remaining raw encodings of `mftb` or other time-base instructions outside `OSReset.s` and `mpc_7xx_603e.s`.
Check whether any generated assembly still uses zero-based `.L_0000...` labels where linked-address labels would improve relocation/comment accuracy.
